metadata.notes: One catalog row per US broadcast/cable parent company. Brands share these specs unless a separate delivery option is listed (different workflow or published document). OTT apps (Peacock, Max, Paramount+, Disney+) have their own catalog entries — linked via relatedServiceIds.

services.name: NBCUniversal
services.tagline: Broadcast, cable, studio & Peacock ingest (parent company)
services.description: NBCUniversal TechOps publishes one inbound specification for longform delivery to NBC broadcast, cable networks, studio servicing, international, and Peacock. Linear brands do not publish separate public program specs — onboarding is contract-gated via TechOps.
distributionKinds: OTT (Peacock — see also Peacock catalog entry)
services.accessModel: NBCUniversal TechOps onboarding — Aspera, Signiant, or Signiant Media Shuttle
services.revenueModel: Ad-supported linear, cable affiliate carriage, and Peacock SVOD/AVOD tiers
businessModelNotes: Peacock Free, Premium, and Premium Plus use the same longform mezzanine path documented in TechOps — ad tiers are a playback/business layer.
Short-form NBCU digital ad creative uses separate NBCU programmatic guidelines (not the longform TechOps mezzanine).
On-air MPEG-2 MXF playout for network transmit is created downstream by NBCU operations — partners typically deliver ProRes/XDCAM mezzanine per TechOps.

deliveryOptions.name: Linear / cable / studio program (ProRes 422 HQ)
deliveryOptions.purpose: Finished episodic and longform to NBCU TechOps
deliveryOptions.summary: Primary HD deliverable for NBC, cable nets, and studio servicing: ProRes 422 HQ in QuickTime MOV at native frame rate and aspect ratio.

videoSpecs.label: Resolution
videoSpecs.value: 1920 × 1080 HD; UHD/HDR in separate document sections

videoSpecs.label: Color
videoSpecs.value: Rec. 709 with required QuickTime NCLC tags

audioSpecs.label: Loudness
audioSpecs.value: Per TechOps audio section (ATSC A/85 aligned)

containerSpecs.label: HDR
containerSpecs.value: HDR-10 ProRes 4444 and Dolby Vision sections require paired SDR

containerSpecs.label: Delivery
containerSpecs.value: Aspera P2P, Signiant P2P, or Signiant Media Shuttle after test-file QC

deliveryOptions.name: Linear / cable program (XDCAM 50 Mbps)
deliveryOptions.purpose: Second-choice HD mezzanine when ProRes is unavailable
deliveryOptions.summary: XDCAM HD422 50 Mbps accepted as second choice for Network, Cable & Studio delivery.

containerSpecs.label: Note
containerSpecs.value: Same onboarding and metadata XLSX as ProRes path

services.name: Paramount Global
services.tagline: CBS broadcast, Viacom cable on-air MXF & commercial traffic
services.description: Paramount Global publishes distinct delivery specs for cable/broadcast on-air MXF (VMN US Media Delivery) versus Paramount+ and Pluto OTT ingest. CBS network commercials use a separate integration manual. All paths require partner or ad-traffic onboarding.
services.accessModel: Paramount ad traffic / Studio Content Operations onboarding
services.revenueModel: Broadcast affiliate, cable MVPD, and Paramount streaming tiers
businessModelNotes: VMN US Media Delivery covers many Viacom/Paramount cable brands for on-air MXF — not full episodic acquisition (see Paramount+ for SVOD mezzanine).
CBS program series delivery to affiliates is deal-specific; VMN HD longform MXF reflects common cable/broadcast playout file format.
CBS Television Network commercial integration is spot/promo traffic only — not series masters.

deliveryOptions.name: Cable / broadcast on-air HD longform (100 Mbps I-frame)
deliveryOptions.purpose: Archive and on-air HD longform for VMN cable brands
deliveryOptions.summary: MXF OP1a (SMPTE 378M) with MPEG-2 4:2:2 High Level, 100 Mbps I-frame CBR at 1080i59.94.

videoSpecs.label: Encoding
videoSpecs.value: MPEG-2 4:2:2 Profile @ High Level, 100 Mbps I-frame CBR

audioSpecs.label: Channels
audioSpecs.value: 4-, 12-, or 16-channel layouts in one MXF track

containerSpecs.label: Timecode
containerSpecs.value: Continuous SMPTE drop-frame in VANC per RP188

containerSpecs.label: Captions
containerSpecs.value: Closed caption data per VMN document

deliveryOptions.name: Cable on-air HD shortform (50 Mbps long-GOP)
deliveryOptions.purpose: Commercials, promos, interstitials on VMN cable infrastructure
deliveryOptions.summary: MXF OP1a with MPEG-2 50 Mbps long-GOP CBR at 1080i59.94 for short-form material.

videoSpecs.label: Encoding
videoSpecs.value: MPEG-2 4:2:2 High Level, 50 Mbps long-GOP CBR

audioSpecs.label: Channels
audioSpecs.value: 2-, 4-, 12-, or 16-channel PCM in one MXF track

containerSpecs.label: Use
containerSpecs.value: Spots, promos, interstitial material — not primary series mezzanine

deliveryOptions.name: CBS broadcast network — commercial integration (HD file)
deliveryOptions.purpose: CBS Television Network spots and PSAs (not series programs)
deliveryOptions.summary: CBS distributes in 1920×1080 HD at 59.94 fields/sec; prefers file delivery via approved commercial delivery services with Ad-ID metadata.

videoSpecs.label: Delivery
videoSpecs.value: File via approved commercial delivery services (HD required; SD only if no HD exists)

videoSpecs.label: Safe area
videoSpecs.value: Center-safe action recommended for downstream center-cut SD

audioSpecs.label: Note
audioSpecs.value: See Appendix A technical specifications in CBS Commercial Integration Manual

containerSpecs.label: Scope
containerSpecs.value: Commercials/PSAs only — not episodic program masters

deliveryOptions.ffmpegNotes: CBS commercial file formats are defined in manual Appendix A — confirm with ad traffic before encoding.

services.name: Warner Bros. Discovery
services.tagline: CNN, Discovery, TNT, HBO linear & licensed cable ingest
services.description: WBD Content Partner Hub defines licensed SDR/HDR mezzanine ingest for cable and linear brands plus Max (DTC). CNN, Discovery, TLC, HGTV, TNT, TBS, and other networks share the Licensed Content SDR specification when delivered via portal or Automated Content Delivery (ACD) with MovieLabs MMC metadata.
services.accessModel: WBD Content Partner Hub or ACD — onboarding required
services.revenueModel: Cable affiliate, ad-supported linear, and Max/discovery+ subscriptions
businessModelNotes: Max Originals and Licensed Content may reference separate contribution spec sections on Partner Hub.
Marketing/promo materials use a different Aspera path (WM Marketing Delivery) than longform programs.
DNxHD and XDCAM mezzanine require approved exceptions on the Licensed SDR spec.

deliveryOptions.name: Cable / linear licensed program (ProRes 422 HQ)
deliveryOptions.purpose: Finished programs to WBD media supply chain for linear and library
deliveryOptions.summary: ProRes 422 HQ is a primary approved SDR master format on the WBD Licensed Content specification.

videoSpecs.label: Resolution
videoSpecs.value: HD 1920×1080 native; SD upconverted with pillarbox

containerSpecs.label: Delivery
containerSpecs.value: Secure portal upload or ACD with MMC XML after full package upload

containerSpecs.label: Composition
containerSpecs.value: Episodic slate + program; theatrical continuous (no ad breaks)

deliveryOptions.name: Cable / linear licensed program (AVC-Intra MXF)
deliveryOptions.summary: AVC-Intra in MXF OP1a at CBR — approved SDR ingest format on Partner Hub.

containerSpecs.label: Note
containerSpecs.value: Same spec family as Max OTT ingest — MMC metadata identifies target network in VersionIntent

deliveryOptions.ffmpegNotes: AVC-Intra MXF requires broadcast authoring tools.

services.name: Disney Entertainment
services.tagline: ABC broadcast, FX, Nat Geo, Freeform linear specs
services.description: Disney Entertainment Television operates ABC broadcast and cable brands. ABC publishes HD commercial (interstitial) MXF specs publicly; episodic program masters for ABC and cable nets are contract-gated through Disney content operations. Disney+ OTT ingest is cataloged separately.
brands: ESPN linear (separate ops specs — contact ESPN Media)
distributionKinds: Cable entertainment (FX, Nat Geo, Disney Channel, etc.)
services.accessModel: Disney content operations / Disney Advertising for ABC spots
services.revenueModel: Broadcast affiliate, cable MVPD, and Disney+ subscription
businessModelNotes: ABC HD commercial spec covers commercials, promos, and PSAs — not series program masters.
ESPN linear and ESPN-on-ABC spots may reference ESPN/ABC companion guidelines; full ESPN series delivery is not in the ABC commercial PDF.
We could not find a single public Disney cable program mezzanine PDF covering FX/Nat Geo — requirements are issued per deal.

deliveryOptions.name: ABC broadcast — HD commercial / interstitial (720p MXF)
deliveryOptions.purpose: Commercials, promos, PSAs for ABC Television Network
deliveryOptions.summary: 1280×720p59.94 progressive MXF compatible with ABC Nexio playout; XDCAM-HD class encoding at 50 Mbps per Disney Advertising spec.

videoSpecs.label: Container
videoSpecs.value: MXF for Harris Nexio server playout

videoSpecs.label: Encoding
videoSpecs.value: XDCAM-HD class, 50 Mbps (per ABC HD Tech Specs)

audioSpecs.label: Codec
audioSpecs.value: Uncompressed PCM, 48 kHz (20-bit or higher per spec)

containerSpecs.label: Metadata
containerSpecs.value: XML metadata file required with delivery

containerSpecs.label: Scope
containerSpecs.value: Interstitial material only — not ABC series program masters

services.name: Fox Corporation
services.tagline: Fox Broadcast, Fox News, FS1 — on-air MXF
services.description: Fox Network Operations publishes separate technical documents for broadcast program material (720p59.94) versus commercial material (1080i59.94 in the 2024 Network Operations spec). Fox News, Fox Business, and Fox Sports (FS1/FS2) use operations-managed ingest — confirm with your Fox distribution contact.
brands: Tubi (FAST/AVOD — digital, not linear MXF spec)
distributionKinds: Cable news & sports (Fox News, FS1, etc.)
services.accessModel: Fox Network Operations / affiliate traffic onboarding
services.revenueModel: Broadcast affiliate, cable MVPD, and Tubi AVOD
businessModelNotes: Program material: 1280×720/59.94 MPEG-2 50 Mbps MXF OP1a (2018 broadcast program spec).
Commercial material (2024): 1920×1080/59.94 MPEG-2 50 Mbps — verify which document your traffic ID requires.
Tubi consumer streaming is not covered by Fox Network Operations MXF PDFs.

deliveryOptions.name: Fox broadcast — program material (720p MXF)
deliveryOptions.purpose: Series and longform program segments to Fox Networks
deliveryOptions.summary: MXF OP1a with MPEG-2 1280×720/59.94, 4:2:2 High Level, 50 Mbps; 8ch PCM; −24 LKFS program loudness.

containerSpecs.label: Document
containerSpecs.value: Technical Specifications for Broadcast Program Material (Aug 2018)

deliveryOptions.name: Fox Networks — commercial material (1080i MXF)
deliveryOptions.summary: MXF OP1a with MPEG-2 1920×1080/59.94, 50 Mbps, 8ch PCM; −24 LKFS per segment; drop-frame timecode.

audioSpecs.label: Channels
audioSpecs.value: 8ch PCM; 5.1 layout per document channel map

containerSpecs.label: Peaks
containerSpecs.value: Instantaneous peaks ≤ −6 dBFS; average peaks ≤ −10 dBFS

services.name: A+E Global Media
services.description: A+E Global Media publishes delivery requirements for Producer Suite partners on the Producer Suite Technical Specifications hub. All listed cable brands share one ProRes mezzanine and 12-channel audio layout delivered via Aspera.
services.accessModel: A+E Producer Suite onboarding — Aspera delivery
businessModelNotes: Technical specifications and deliverables (including Production Delivery Technical Requirements) are published on Producer Suite — no per-brand public spec splits.

deliveryOptions.name: Cable program master (ProRes 422 HQ, 12-track audio)
deliveryOptions.purpose: Finished episodes and specials to A+E Global Media
deliveryOptions.summary: 1080 ProRes 422 HQ with documented 12-channel PCM layout; Aspera transfer after Producer Suite onboarding.

audioSpecs.label: Layout
audioSpecs.value: Up to 12-channel PCM per Production Delivery Technical Requirements
